description: Section 02 — now. Cream-2 background. Left rail of clickable tabs (numbered 01..N) controls a panel on the right that swaps body + chips. Side aside lists practical examples.

fields.description: Mono line above the side bullets.
fields.name: side_intro
fields.title: Side intro

name: CV Now / AI

test_data.heading: AI-native, on top of a stack that already works.

items.body: Build and ship Model Context Protocol servers that plug company data, internal APIs and tooling into Claude / Cursor / agent runtimes. The bridge between LLMs and the messy real world.
items.label: MCP servers

items.body: Multi-step agents for support, ops, content and code review. Tool use, guardrails, evals, human-in-the-loop. Boring on purpose — they have to actually work on Monday morning.
items.label: Agent workflows

items.body: Hybrid retrieval over PDFs, ticketing systems, CRMs and 130+ legacy WordPress installs. Postgres + pgvector when sane, dedicated vector DBs when not.
items.label: RAG + search

items.body: Yes, really. Symfony + LLM gateways, streaming responses into WooCommerce, content pipelines for WordPress at scale. AI on top of the stack you already paid for.
items.label: LLM in PHP

test_data.side_intro: // what this looks like in practice

side_items.text: An MCP server that lets Claude hit your Symfony admin API directly.
A support agent that reads tickets, drafts replies, tags them, and shuts up when it isn't sure.
A WordPress plugin that uses an LLM for content ops without leaking your data.
RAG over a decade of project docs so the next dev doesn't start from zero.

test_data.sub: The last decade was PHP, Symfony, WordPress, React. The next one is all of that plus agents, MCP and LLMs doing real work — not demos.
